title: Willingness-to-Pay Segmentation

content.statement: Different users value the same thing differently.
content.question: Which segment of your business would pay 2x if you offered them something slightly different?
content.equation: WTPs = Σ(Segment Size × WTP × Purchase Prob × Margin)
sop: Break your market into 3-4 segments by need, urgency, or identity, not just demographics.
Estimate Segment Size, number of potential customers in each group (see calculation, page 6).
Estimate willingness to pay and Purchase Probability for each segment (see calculation, page 6).
Estimate Margin per sale for each segment (see calculation, page 6).

Multiply Segment Size, willingness to pay, Purchase Probability, and Margin for each segment (see calculation, page 6), and build a dedicated offer for whichever segment produces the highest expected value.
content.explainer: One price fits few. Segment by willingness-to-pay to capture value that's sitting on the table.
questions: Which segment of your business would pay 2x if you offered them something slightly different?
Are you charging your highest-willingness-to-pay customers the same price as everyone else, and if so, how much money is that leaving behind?
What's the smallest, highest-value segment in your customer base that you've never built a dedicated offer for?

definitions.def: Willingness to pay, the maximum price a specific customer or segment would realistically pay for what you offer.
The average payoff of an uncertain bet, with each possible outcome weighted by how likely it is. Lets you compare options that each have different odds and rewards.

content.equation_example: Segment Size 500 customers, WTP $40, Purchase Probability 30%, Margin 50%. 500 times $40 times 0.30 times 0.50 equals $3,000 expected profit contribution. Higher means that segment is worth building a dedicated offer for. Lower means it's not worth the extra effort to target separately.
content.closing_statement: Different customers are worth different amounts to you.

caption:
Principle Nº 64: Willingness-to-Pay Segmentation

Different users value the same thing differently. One price fits few.

Ask yourself: Which segment of your business would pay 2x if you offered them something slightly different?
